meta.tagline: Curated knowledge for family, learning, and development

items.title: 7 AI Prompts to Build the Two Claude Projects Your Business Is Missing
items.excerpt: Build two Claude Projects this weekend: a sales pipeline that drafts proposals in 20 minutes and a strategic advisor that knows your goals, your numbers, and your business. 7 copy-paste prompts included.
items.summary: Article provides seven copy-paste prompts to create Claude AI projects for automated proposal drafting and strategic business advising. The tutorial promises to help users build functional AI assistants that can complete sales proposals in 20 minutes and provide business guidance based on specific goals and metrics.
items.pullQuote: Build functional AI assistants that draft proposals in 20 minutes

items.title: Anthropic Asked 81,000 People What They Actually Want From AI.
items.excerpt: The Answers Should Sound Familiar. Anthropic published something that most AI coverage will miss the point on.
items.summary: Anthropic conducted large-scale user research with 81,000 participants to understand actual AI user preferences and needs. The research reveals insights about consumer AI expectations that differ from typical industry coverage and assumptions.
items.pullQuote: 81,000 participants reveal what users actually want from AI

items.title: What No One Tells You About Being a Polymath
items.excerpt: Why you need to pause and intentionally consider being one
items.summary: Article explores the hidden challenges and benefits of developing expertise across multiple disciplines. The piece argues for intentionally considering a polymathic approach to learning and intellectual development.
items.pullQuote: Intentionally consider developing expertise across multiple disciplines

items.title: MLB Spring Breakout 2026: Top Prospects, Pitches & Performances From This Year's Games
items.excerpt: Baseball America was on hand for every 2026 MLB Spring Breakout Game. Here are the top prospects and plays we saw. The post MLB Spring Breakout 2026: Top Prospects, Pitches & Performances From This Year’s Games appeared first on College Baseball, MLB Draft, Prospects - Baseball America.
items.summary: Baseball America provided comprehensive coverage of the 2026 MLB Spring Breakout games, highlighting top prospects and standout performances. The event showcased emerging talent with detailed analysis of pitching mechanics and game execution at the professional development level.
items.pullQuote: Top prospects showcased advanced pitching mechanics and standout performances

items.title: #385 – AMA #82: Applying the tools of longevity in the real world: disease prevention, DEXA scans, artificial sweeteners, injury recovery, stability training, habit formation, protein intake and mTOR activation, and more
items.excerpt: “Everything we're doing is simultaneously geared towards reducing the risk of chronic disease but also improving healthspan." —Peter Attia The post #385 – AMA #82: Applying the tools of longevity in the real world: disease prevention, DEXA scans, artificial sweeteners, injury recovery, stability training, habit formation, protein intake and mTOR activation, and more appeared first on Peter Attia.
items.summary: Dr. Peter Attia discusses practical implementation of longevity strategies including DEXA body composition scanning, protein intake optimization, and stability training for injury prevention. The episode covers real-world applications of health optimization tools that simultaneously reduce chronic disease risk while improving functional capacity.
items.pullQuote: protein intake and mTOR activation strategies for optimal body composition

items.title: Where One Might Look
items.excerpt: If frames shape conscious life, they should also leave empirical traces.
items.summary: Neuroscientist Bernard Baars explores how cognitive frameworks that shape conscious experience should leave detectable empirical traces in brain activity. The piece examines the intersection of consciousness research and measurable neurological patterns.
items.pullQuote: frames shape conscious life through measurable empirical traces

items.title: Last Call for paid membership ends tonight
items.excerpt: 4 hours left at the current price. This is the last window to lock in your current rate.
items.summary: FinStory AI is offering final hours to subscribe at current pricing before rates increase. The newsletter focuses on artificial intelligence applications in financial markets and business analysis.
items.pullQuote: Final window to lock in current subscription rate expires tonight

items.title: Why He'll Surrender Soon
items.excerpt: But he’ll call it a great victory
items.summary: Political analyst Robert Reich predicts Trump will soon surrender on an unspecified issue but will frame the capitulation as a victory. The article appears to focus on political messaging strategies and Trump's approach to managing public perception.
items.pullQuote: He'll call it a great victory despite surrendering

items.title: Sunday caption contest: Drowning
items.summary: Robert Reich hosts a weekly caption contest with a political cartoon featuring a drowning theme. The post also announces the winner from the previous week's contest.
items.pullQuote: Weekly political cartoon caption contest with drowning theme

items.title: DOGE Dumbed Down the Nation (w/ Jessica Riedl)
items.excerpt: Brookings scholar Jessica Riedl analyzes the DOGE debacle and the failure of Americans to make grown up choices about spending and taxes.
items.summary: Brookings Institution scholar Jessica Riedl analyzes the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) initiative and its impact on public discourse. She argues that Americans have failed to make mature decisions about the relationship between government spending and taxation.
items.pullQuote: Americans failing to make grown up choices about spending

items.title: MAGA's Mueller Myths
items.excerpt: The 7 lies that Trumpists still cling to.
items.summary: The Bulwark identifies seven persistent false narratives that Trump supporters maintain about the Mueller investigation into Russian interference. The article appears to fact-check claims about the special counsel's findings and methodology.
items.pullQuote: Seven persistent lies about the Mueller investigation debunked

items.title: GOP Grows More Unpopular, More Desperate
items.excerpt: Feeling cornered, Republicans are willing to try anything but shift to policies Americans want.
items.summary: The article argues that declining Republican popularity is driving increasingly desperate political tactics. Rather than adopting policies that align with public preferences, the GOP is reportedly pursuing alternative strategies to maintain power.
items.pullQuote: Republicans willing to try anything but popular policies

briefing.editorsNote: Today's focus spans from developing multiple talents to optimizing our bodies, with practical wisdom threading through each domain.

bullets.text: Polymaths face unique challenges balancing breadth with depth across multiple intellectual disciplines.
Spring Breakout showcased 2026's top prospects with detailed pitching mechanics and performance analysis.
Dr. Attia shares actionable longevity tools including DEXA scans and protein optimization strategies.
FinStory AI's membership pricing increases tonight for their financial market analysis newsletter.
Scholar critiques DOGE initiative's impact on Americans' understanding of government spending trade-offs.
